speaker: RICHMOND
text: God and your arms be praised, victorious friends!

speaker: RICHMOND
text: The day is ours; the bloody dog is dead.

speaker: STANLEY
text: Courageous Richmond, well hast thou acquit thee.

speaker: STANLEY
text: Lo, here this long-usurpèd royalty

speaker: STANLEY
text: From the dead temples of this bloody wretch

speaker: STANLEY
text: Have I plucked off, to grace thy brows withal.

speaker: STANLEY
text: Wear it, enjoy it, and make much of it.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Great God of heaven, say amen to all!

speaker: RICHMOND
text: But tell me, is young George Stanley living?

speaker: STANLEY
text: He is, my lord, and safe in Leicester town,

speaker: STANLEY
text: Whither, if it please you, we may now withdraw us.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: What men of name are slain on either side?

speaker: STANLEY
text: John, Duke of Norfolk, Walter, Lord Ferrers,

speaker: STANLEY
text: Sir Robert Brakenbury, and Sir William Brandon.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Inter their bodies as becomes their births.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Proclaim a pardon to the soldiers fled

speaker: RICHMOND
text: That in submission will return to us.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: And then, as we have ta’en the sacrament,

speaker: RICHMOND
text: We will unite the white rose and the red;

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Smile heaven upon this fair conjunction,

speaker: RICHMOND
text: That long have frowned upon their enmity.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: What traitor hears me and says not “Amen”?

speaker: RICHMOND
text: England hath long been mad and scarred herself:

speaker: RICHMOND
text: The brother blindly shed the brother’s blood;

speaker: RICHMOND
text: The father rashly slaughtered his own son;

speaker: RICHMOND
text: The son, compelled, been butcher to the sire.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: All this divided York and Lancaster,

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Divided in their dire division.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: O, now let Richmond and Elizabeth,

speaker: RICHMOND
text: The true succeeders of each royal house,

speaker: RICHMOND
text: By God’s fair ordinance conjoin together,

speaker: RICHMOND
text: And let their heirs, God, if Thy will be so,

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Enrich the time to come with smooth-faced peace,

speaker: RICHMOND
text: With smiling plenty and fair prosperous days.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Abate the edge of traitors, gracious Lord,

speaker: RICHMOND
text: That would reduce these bloody days again

speaker: RICHMOND
text: And make poor England weep in streams of blood.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Let them not live to taste this land’s increase,

speaker: RICHMOND
text: That would with treason wound this fair land’s peace.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Now civil wounds are stopped, peace lives again.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: That she may long live here, God say amen.
